context: Checks whether the agent can undo overengineering and test theater while retaining only evidence-backed changes needed for the defect.

checklist.name: Root cause and minimal patch
checklist.description: Reconstructs the original defect from the failing behavior and proposes the smallest production change that fixes it.

checklist.name: Defect test restored
checklist.description: Restores or replaces the original failing test with a behavior-level regression that fails before and passes after the fix.

checklist.name: Abstraction inflation rejected
checklist.description: Challenges one-implementation interfaces, trivial builders, and ceremonial mapper layers unless a concrete boundary/variation exists.

checklist.name: Build/dependency/format churn removed
checklist.description: Separates and reverts unrelated framework/JDK upgrades, dependency additions, and repository-wide formatting from the defect.

checklist.name: Outcome-focused tests
checklist.description: Rejects mocking the subject under test and brittle interaction assertions in favor of outcomes and real boundary behavior.

checklist.name: Unchanged behavior proof
checklist.description: Protects nearby null/non-null, error, side-effect, and public contract paths rather than only the happy path.

checklist.name: Diff-scope guard
checklist.description: Uses or emulates a changed-file/line review to detect generated files, public API changes, deleted tests, migrations, and build-code coupling.

checklist.name: Exact verification
checklist.description: Requires named wrapper commands and captured results; it does not repeat the unsubstantiated all-checks-pass claim.

checklist.name: Heuristics are not proof
checklist.description: Uses size/smell/static-analysis signals to guide inspection but ties final findings to code, tests, contracts, or build evidence.
